title: Turning Conversation Into Action (Palantir CSE, #2)

extractedEntities.name: Customer Service Engine
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI-enabled Customer Service Engine (CSE) that deploys AI agents with operational context to automate customer service operations end-to-end

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir AIP platform used to set up guard-railed access to data, custom-built tools, actions, and human feedback capture for AI agents

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir Foundry platform through which data pipelines hydrate connected objects representing enterprise concepts like Customers, Orders, and Products

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology primitive that allows creation of custom behaviors and actions that AI agents can execute with proper permissions, including object edits, external API calls, and code execution via Function on Objects

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Connected objects in the Ontology representing enterprise concepts such as Customers, Orders, Products, and Queries, backed by source systems like SAP and Salesforce

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Function on Objects capability enabling free-form code execution as part of actions within the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Agent Isolation
extractedEntities.description: Design pattern where each AI agent is built for a specific purpose and configured to access specific views of the connected Ontology, navigating different paths through the object landscape at runtime

extractedEntities.name: Agentic Architecture
extractedEntities.description: Multi-agent architecture with classification agent, domain-specific agents (ETA, order management), master agent, and communication agent working together to process customer queries

extractedEntities.name: Human-in-the-Loop
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where human operators validate AI-suggested actions, provide feedback on incorrect suggestions, and handle escalated cases that require human judgment

extractedEntities.name: ObjectSet
extractedEntities.description: Specific views of the connected Ontology that agents are configured to access, representing filtered subsets of objects relevant to each agent's purpose

extractedEntities.name: Agent Data Navigation
extractedEntities.description: At runtime, agents navigate different paths through the object landscape to read information, following links between objects like Order to Customer to Shipping Information

extractedEntities.name: Customer Service Automation
extractedEntities.description: AI agents autonomously handle customer service queries including delivery ETA inquiries, order cancellations, and other customer experience processes with human validation

keyInsights: Actions are a first-class Ontology primitive that can drive in-platform changes (object edits), external changes (API calls), and free-form code execution (Function on Objects)
AI agents operate on specific views of the connected Ontology rather than having unrestricted access, implementing an agent isolation pattern for security and relevance

The multi-agent architecture includes specialized roles: classification agent for routing, domain-specific agents for processing, master agent for coordination, and communication agent for response drafting
Objects in the Ontology are hydrated through data pipelines from source systems like SAP, Salesforce, ERP systems, and data warehouses
Human-in-the-loop feedback is captured when agents suggest incorrect actions, enabling continuous improvement of the AI system
Agents navigate linked objects at runtime to gather information across the Ontology graph, such as following Order to Customer to Shipping Information paths

title: User-Centered Machine Learning

extractedEntities.name: UCML
extractedEntities.description: User-Centered Machine Learning — Palantir's solution that transforms ML models from isolated entities into live, dynamic solutions that users can improve in seconds through real-time feedback

extractedEntities.name: Real-Time Model Refinement
extractedEntities.description: Pattern enabling users to provide feedback to computer vision models in real-time, improving model output on-the-fly within the context of the mission rather than waiting for slow retrain cycles

extractedEntities.name: Computer Vision Object Detection
extractedEntities.description: Application of CV models on overhead aerial imagery for detecting and tracking objects of interest, including military vehicles, vessels, and aircraft on satellite imagery

extractedEntities.name: Object Detection Accuracy
extractedEntities.description: In realistic deployment conditions, observed accuracy of object detection and tracking models is usually in the range of 60-80 percent, requiring user interaction to improve results

extractedEntities.name: User Feedback Loop
extractedEntities.description: Workflow pattern where users interact with model detections to provide corrections, which the model uses to improve in real-time, fundamentally redefining analyst workflows

extractedEntities.name: Defense Intelligence Analysis
extractedEntities.description: DoD use case where imagery analysts use CV models to detect and track objects across satellite electro-optical imagery and full motion video feeds for strategic intelligence

keyInsights: UCML transforms models from walled-off entities trained in slow cycles into live solutions that users can improve in seconds through real-time feedback
The key problem with CV models is not accuracy alone but the inability for users to interact with and refine model output within mission context
Real-time user feedback to models represents a fundamental paradigm shift from static detection workflows to interactive, dynamic analysis workflows

title: Using Static Code Analysis to Improve Log Parsing

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir Foundry platform whose backend and frontend components emit structured JSON log lines for debugging and operational monitoring

extractedEntities.name: Gotham
extractedEntities.description: Palantir Gotham platform whose components emit structured JSON log lines alongside Foundry for the shared logging infrastructure

extractedEntities.name: Structured Log Format
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's standard JSON log format with type, message, level, params, and time fields that avoids string formatting of parameters into messages, enabling structured indexing and querying

extractedEntities.name: Planer
extractedEntities.description: Palantir tool that uses regular expressions to impose standard log structure on unstructured log lines from third-party software, enabling indexing through the same log pipeline

extractedEntities.name: Rubix
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's name for their Kubernetes infrastructure that runs third-party software like etcd, vault, and Kubernetes itself

extractedEntities.name: Static Code Analysis for Logs
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of combining regular expressions and static code analysis to extract structured information from otherwise unstructured third-party logs for unified debugging workflows

keyInsights: Palantir's logging infrastructure uses structured JSON format with parameterized messages rather than string-formatted messages, enabling SQL-like querying and aggregation for debugging
The Rubix team (Kubernetes infrastructure) uses static code analysis combined with regex to parse third-party software logs into Palantir's structured format
Structured logging enables product teams to become operationally responsible for their software across hundreds of installations, making support tractable for small teams

title: A Better Conversation (Palantir CSE, #1)

extractedEntities.name: Customer Service Engine
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI-enabled Customer Service Engine (CSE) designed around an Ontology tailored to partner operations, achieving 90 percent accurate resolution of customer service cases within six weeks

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: The core data model in Palantir tailored to specific partner operations, providing the operational context that enables AI agents to understand and act on enterprise data and processes

extractedEntities.name: Ontology-Anchored AI
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where AI agents are grounded in the Ontology's operational context rather than operating on raw data, ensuring accurate and contextual responses tied to real business objects and processes

extractedEntities.name: Human Feedback Loop
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where complex cases are escalated to human agents, creating a continuous learning and improvement cycle for the CSE rather than learning from production mistakes

extractedEntities.name: Manufacturing Customer Service
extractedEntities.description: Deployment at a vertically integrated manufacturing, wholesale, and retail company handling invoice inquiries, product information, tracking details, and warranty claims

extractedEntities.name: Agentic Evaluation
extractedEntities.description: Robust evaluation methodology for AI agents that validates accuracy and consistency of autonomous processes before deployment to production

extractedEntities.name: CSE Performance Metrics
extractedEntities.description: Over 90 percent of queries handled within 6 weeks, resolution actions in under 10 seconds (down from 20 minute average), 75 percent reduction in repeat queries

keyInsights: The CSE is designed around an Ontology tailored specifically to partner operations, providing agents with the operational context needed for accurate resolution rather than generic chat capabilities
Blanket automation of customer support without human feedback is detrimental because the system learns by making mistakes in production, whereas human-in-the-loop enables controlled improvement
The CSE achieved under 10-second resolution time compared to the previous 20-minute average, demonstrating the efficiency gain of Ontology-anchored AI agents

Customer service AI requires a bespoke approach for each organization because the unique relationship between customers and organizations means one-size-fits-all solutions are neither feasible nor desirable

title: A Day in the Life of a Palantir Forward Deployed Software Engineer

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir Foundry platform used by FDSEs to configure and deploy data integration solutions for customer-specific use cases across cyber, healthcare, and defense

extractedEntities.name: Gotham
extractedEntities.description: Palantir Gotham platform used alongside Foundry by FDSEs as ready-built playgrounds for composing customer solutions

extractedEntities.name: Forward Deployed Engineering
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's model where Forward Deployed Software Engineers (FDSEs or Deltas) embed directly with customers to configure existing platforms to solve specific problems, enabling many capabilities for a single customer

extractedEntities.name: Data Integration Solutions
extractedEntities.description: FDSE deployment focused on delivering data integration solutions for US Department of Defense customers using Foundry and Gotham

keyInsights: FDSEs configure Palantir's existing products rather than building from scratch, enabling rapid solution delivery by composing the right architecture of features for each customer
The FDSE model distinguishes Palantir from consulting: solutions are composed from ready-built platform capabilities rather than custom-built patchwork solutions
FDSEs work across multiple domains (cyber, healthcare, defense) leveraging the same platform, demonstrating Foundry and Gotham's domain-agnostic nature

title: Beyond Founder Mode: Mission Mode

extractedEntities.name: Mission Mode
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's organizational philosophy of organizing the entire company around mission-critical customer work rather than founder involvement, ensuring every team member operates with founder-level intensity driven by the mission

extractedEntities.name: Palantir
extractedEntities.description: Technology company that has operated in Mission Mode for two decades, building software for healthcare, defense, intelligence, and critical infrastructure

keyInsights: Mission Mode organizes the company around the question of what is the most important work customers are doing and how to maximize their success, rather than around internal organizational hierarchy
Palantir builds software that operates in austere edge environments with unreliable connectivity, reflecting the mission-critical nature of deployment contexts
The willingness to work on hard problems that other companies consider not scalable is a deliberate cultural choice driven by mission orientation

title: Building the Customer-Centric Bank: Drawing up a tech blueprint

extractedEntities.name: Operational Data Connectivity
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's approach to connecting data across the customer lifecycle by replacing one-way assembly line data flows with bidirectional, integrated data architecture that enables real-time response to changes

extractedEntities.name: Customer-Centric Banking
extractedEntities.description: Use case where banks deploy data-driven solutions considering customer needs, goals, and life stages across sales campaigns, onboarding, and servicing using connected operational data

extractedEntities.name: Customer 360
extractedEntities.description: Traditional pattern of cascading data through single client views, entity resolution layers, and Customer-360 tooling into discrete workflows, which Palantir argues is too slow and functionally disjointed

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Data architecture approach that goes beyond traditional Customer-360 by providing operational connectivity across the full customer lifecycle, enabling banks to respond to behavioral changes in real time

keyInsights: Traditional Customer-360 and single client view approaches create one-way assembly line data flows that are too slow and functionally disjointed for true customer centricity
Operational data connectivity replaces discrete, cascading data flows with an integrated architecture that enables bidirectional data use across sales, onboarding, and servicing
Customer-facing solutions must not be alienated from one another — the Ontology provides the shared business understanding that connects them

title: Customer-Centric Transformation at a European Bank

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir Foundry platform used for over half a decade to help a major European bank position customer value at the core of its transformation strategy through integrated data assets

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: The resulting data asset at Palantir that goes far beyond a single client view to collate data, analytics, and the workflows they inform into a common business understanding

extractedEntities.name: Foundational Data Asset
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of building a holistic customer data asset by integrating CRM, ERP, core banking systems, martech analytics, and credit/risk profiles into a unified Ontology before layering use-case-specific functionality

extractedEntities.name: Banking Upsell Campaigns
extractedEntities.description: European bank use case where integrated Ontology enabled sales and marketing teams to launch daily personalized campaigns with 15 percent improvement in response rates

extractedEntities.name: Data Source Integration
extractedEntities.description: Integration of CRM data, martech stack analytics (physical and digital channels), credit/risk profiles, and core banking systems into a unified Ontology for customer intelligence

extractedEntities.name: Prove Then Scale
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's approach of establishing a focused initial use case to prove value before expanding scope and ambition of the data platform deployment

keyInsights: The Ontology goes beyond a single client view by collating data, analytics, and the workflows they inform into a common business understanding that teams can operate on directly
Building the Ontology starts with integrating data from multiple source systems (CRM, ERP, core banking) with stringent access and governance controls as a foundational layer
The initial upsell use case was delivered in weeks rather than months/years, demonstrating the rapid time-to-value of the Foundry Ontology approach
Response rates improved by 15 percent through greater personalization enabled by the unified Ontology view of customers across all data sources

title: Data is the New Snake Oil

extractedEntities.name: Palantir
extractedEntities.description: Technology company that builds software for institutions with complex data environments, advocating for decision-centric rather than prediction-centric approaches to digital transformation

extractedEntities.name: Decision-Centric Transformation
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's guiding principle that digital transformation should focus on improving operational decisions across the whole business model rather than optimizing predictions for single metrics

extractedEntities.name: Solve the Right Problem
extractedEntities.description: Principle that most companies should invest in decision-making capabilities rather than prediction models, since prediction optimizes one slice while decisions optimize the whole business

extractedEntities.name: Follow the Right Path
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's principle for effective transformation: solve the right problem and follow the right path, avoiding the trap of buying tools and services without strategic alignment

keyInsights: Most companies should invest in improving operational decisions rather than building prediction models, since prediction optimizes one slice while decision optimization improves the whole business model
Digital transformation done poorly is an exercise in buying tools and services; done well, it leverages unique organizational strengths to evolve the business
The Google/Netflix prediction model (predicting clicks/views) does not transfer to most enterprises where the core challenge is complex operational decision-making across many variables

title: Engineering for Impact

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir Foundry deployed as a single source of truth for the UK NHS COVID-19 response, supporting vaccine supply chain optimization and elective recovery

extractedEntities.name: NHS COVID-19 Response
extractedEntities.description: Deployment of Palantir Foundry as a single source of truth for the UK NHS to manage COVID-19 response including infection spread tracking, critical care capacity monitoring, and vaccine supply chain optimization

extractedEntities.name: Single Source of Truth
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of deploying Foundry as the authoritative data platform that provides a unified view for local and national leadership to see the true picture of complex operational situations

keyInsights: Foundry was deployed as a single source of truth for the NHS COVID-19 response, enabling national leadership to see infection spread and critical care capacity in near real-time
Palantir's approach emphasizes inclusive collaboration with domain experts who have years of experience, rather than coming in as outside consultants to replace existing knowledge
The same Foundry platform was extended from pandemic response to vaccine supply chain optimization and elective recovery, demonstrating platform versatility

title: Evaluating Software (Palantir RFx Blog Series, #0)

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Referenced as a key topic in the RFx blog series for finding meaning in data, positioned as a core component for digital transformation software evaluation

extractedEntities.name: Data Connection
extractedEntities.description: First step in data integration within the Palantir platform, referenced as a key evaluation criterion for digital transformation software

extractedEntities.name: RFx Evaluation Framework
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's structured approach to evaluating digital transformation software through formal solicitations covering Ontology, data connection, pipeline version control, interoperability, operational security, PETs, and AI/ML operationalization

extractedEntities.name: Operational Security
extractedEntities.description: Security framework for enabling transparency, collaboration, and privacy as a core evaluation criterion for digital transformation software

extractedEntities.name: Privacy-Enhancing Technologies
extractedEntities.description: PETs adoption guide referenced as part of the evaluation framework for digital transformation software alongside operational security

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Version Control
extractedEntities.description: Capability for tracking code and data together in data pipeline development, referenced as a key evaluation criterion

keyInsights: Palantir's RFx evaluation framework covers seven key areas: Ontology, Data Connection, Pipeline Version Control, Interoperability, Operational Security, PETs, and AI/ML Operationalization
Data schemas requiring customization are generally more effective and scalable than purely out-of-the-box solutions, challenging the common RFx bias toward turnkey responses
Complex software requirements cannot be adequately evaluated through numerical grading scales; they require demonstrable responses like live demos, past performance, or pilots

title: Announcing Fellowships for Open-Source Developers

extractedEntities.name: Palantir Open Source
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's portfolio of open-source projects spanning Kubernetes internals, TypeScript linting, Python IDEs, and multiple programming languages including Java, Go, TypeScript, and Python

keyInsights: Palantir maintains open-source projects spanning Kubernetes internals, TypeScript linting, and Python IDEs across Java, Go, TypeScript, and Python
Palantir invests in open-source community through fellowship programs for feature development, code reviews, documentation, and community engagement

title: From Fragile to Resilient: Building a connected company

extractedEntities.name: Common Operating Picture
extractedEntities.description: Foundation of operational resilience where basic status information about all operational units is instantly accessible and updating in near real-time, informing strategy and action

extractedEntities.name: Operational Resilience
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's framework for diagnosing and addressing organizational fragility caused by inflexible, disconnected technology architecture with baked-in assumptions

extractedEntities.name: Decision Influence Mechanism
extractedEntities.description: Pattern for introducing additional variables or new objective functions into regular employee decision-making processes with seamless tracking of adherence and efficacy

extractedEntities.name: Supply Chain Resilience
extractedEntities.description: Cross-industry use case for real-time operational visibility covering manufacturing inventory, airline fleet management, media audience forecasting, and oil and gas well operations

keyInsights: Operational fragility is caused by inflexible, disconnected technology architecture fraught with assumptions baked into automation that reflect a simpler world than reality
A Common Operating Picture requires instantly accessible, near real-time status information about all operational units as the foundation for strategy and action
Resilient organizations need mechanisms to influence decision-making processes by introducing new variables or objective functions, with tracking of adherence and efficacy
The three-question self-assessment measures fragility: speed of basic status access, ability to change decision objective functions, and capacity to rapidly adapt processes

title: From Service to Software

extractedEntities.name: Palantir
extractedEntities.description: Technology company with a strong veteran community, attracting military service members who value mission-oriented culture, execution focus, and real-world impact

keyInsights: Palantir's culture of mission-orientation, execution focus, and real-world impact attracts military veterans across diverse roles from customer-facing to software development
The company's veteran community spans multiple branches of military services globally and diverse roles within Palantir

title: Interviewing at Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Palantir
extractedEntities.description: Technology company that evaluates candidates holistically with emphasis on potential for growth, welcoming non-traditional backgrounds, rather than testing for a single perfect fit

keyInsights: Palantir's hiring process focuses on holistic evaluation including growth potential and distinctive qualities rather than testing for a single predefined perfect fit
The company welcomes candidates from non-traditional backgrounds, self-taught disciplines, and career changers, emphasizing journey and innate strengths over credentials
